LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ED ROBINSON'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS IS GRANTED. THE DECLARATION OF ED ROBINSON IN FURTHER SUPPORT OF MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS ESTABLISHES THAT MR. ROBINSON IS NOT A DIRECTOR OF THE ENTITY THAT CONTRACTED WITH PLAINTIFF; RATHER, HE IS A DIRECTOR OF THE CONTRACTING ENTITY'S PARENT COMPANY. (ROBINSON REPLY DECL., 3.) THE COURT DOES NOT HAVE PERSONAL JURISDICTION OVER MR. ROBINSON BECAUSE MR. ROBINSON DOES NOT HAVE SUFFICIENT MINIMUM CONTACTS WITH THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA TO NOT OFFEND THE TRADITIONAL NOTIONS OF FAIR PLAY AND SUBSTANTIAL JUSTICE. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Jun-09-2014 Vacated

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Apr-21-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling which was not contested. PLAINTIFF APPBACKR INC.'s Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint is Granted. No opposition filed. Order signed. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR# 5976.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-05-2014 Set For Trial On Jun-23-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Dec-27-2013 Continued To Feb-19-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar Dec-23-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT ED ROBINSON'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS is Granted. The Declaration of Ed Robinson in Further Support of Motion to Quash Service of Summons establishes that Mr. Robinson is not a director of the entity that contracted with Plaintiff; rather, he is a director of the contracting entity's parent company. (Robinson Reply Decl., 3.) The Court does not have personal jurisdiction over Mr. Robinson because Mr. Robinson does not have sufficient minimum contacts with the state of California to not offend the traditional notions of fair play and substantial justice. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =302/MJM
